seo.title: LlamaIndex Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for LlamaIndex agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using LlamaIndex face heightened compliance risks due to the framework's reliance on retrieved context for LLM responses, where irrelevant retrievals or hallucinations can lead to inaccurate financial advice or exposure of sensitive transaction data. Regulations like SOX, PCI DSS, and SEC rules demand verifiable accuracy, data protection, and explainability in AI-driven decisions. Vex provides essential guardrails by verifying query responses against compliance policies, preventing regulatory violations and financial losses.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over AI systems generating financial insights from indexed data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Verifies response accuracy against source documents, flagging hallucinations or irrelevant retrievals that could misstate financial data.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption and protection of cardholder data in transit and at rest, including RAG systems handling transaction records.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects PII leakage in responses and enforces encryption checks on indexed transaction data.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires data minimization, consent, and right to explanation for automated decisions affecting customers.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Ensures responses only include consented data and provides audit trails for explainability in credit decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Financial advice from AI must include disclaimers and be based on verifiable, up-to-date information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Mandates disclaimer inclusion and validates freshness of indexed market data to prevent outdated advice.

industryRisks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
industryRisks.description: RAG retrieves outdated or unrelated financial docs, leading to incorrect investment recommendations.
industryRisks.example: Query for current stock price returns 2022 earnings report, causing bad trade advice.

industryRisks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
industryRisks.description: LLM fabricates transaction details or compliance status not in indexed sources.
industryRisks.example: Response claims 'no AML flags' when source docs show suspicious activity.

industryRisks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
industryRisks.description: Stale market data index leads to recommendations based on obsolete information.
industryRisks.example: Advises buying bankrupt company's stock due to unrefreshed index.

industryRisks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
industryRisks.description: Document splitting cuts off key financial clauses, omitting risk disclosures.
industryRisks.example: Credit approval response misses 'high-risk borrower' clause split across chunks.

implementation.title: Wrap LlamaIndex Query Engine with Vex Verify (Python)
implementation.description: Protect financial advice queries by verifying relevance, freshness, and including SOX/GDPR disclaimers.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, QueryBundle

from vex import verify

# Load financial docs into index
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('financial_docs/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

@verify(policies=[
    'response.relevance > 0.8',
    'contains_disclaimer("This is not financial advice"),
    'no_pii_leakage()',
    'index_freshness < 24h'
])
def safe_financial_query(query: str):
    return query_engine.query(query)

response = safe_financial_query('What\'s the risk profile of this investment?')

implementation.title: TypeScript Integration for Transaction Data RAG
implementation.description: Secure PCI DSS compliance by redacting card numbers and verifying AML context relevance.

implementation.title: Python Credit Decision Explainability Guardrail
implementation.description: SEC-compliant explainability for credit decisions with staleness and chunk boundary checks.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage
from vex import verify

# Load credit application index
storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir='./credit_index')
index = load_index_from_storage(storage_context)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

@verify(policies=[
    'explainable_decision_factors()',
    'index_staleness < 1h',
    'chunk_context_complete()',  # Checks boundary errors
    'sec_disclaimer_present()'
])
def credit_decision(query: str):
    return query_engine.query(query)

approval = credit_decision('Should we approve this $50k loan application?')

caseStudyScenario.heading: LlamaIndex in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
WealthTrust Bank deployed LlamaIndex to power their AI investment advisor, indexing 10TB of market research, client portfolios, and regulatory filings. The RAG system answered client queries like 'Should I invest in TechFund?' by retrieving relevant fund documents and SEC filings. However, without compliance guardrails, the system suffered from common LlamaIndex failure modes: stale index data from weekend market crashes wasn't refreshed, chunk boundaries split risk disclosures across documents, and the synthesizer occasionally hallucinated performance metrics not present in sources.

In one incident, a high-net-worth client received a recommendation to buy TechFund based on hallucinated '15% YTD returns' (actual: -8%) from an unrefreshed index, plus missing risk warnings cut by chunking errors. The client invested $2M, lost $180k in a market downturn, and filed complaints triggering SEC investigation.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex guardrails around query_engine.query(), WealthTrust achieved 99.7% response compliance. Vex blocked 1,247 risky responses including 312 staleness violations, 189 hallucination attempts, and 76 chunk boundary errors. All advice now includes mandatory SEC disclaimers, PII is automatically redacted per PCI DSS, and explainability traces link decisions to specific indexed sources. The bank avoided $15M in potential fines, retained their banking license, and gained SEC audit clearance.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your LlamaIndex pipelines today to eliminate compliance risks and protect your fintech operations.
